title: Why did Gendou think Yui would be ok with him cheating
display_title: Why did Gendou think Yui would be ok with him cheating
wikitext:
[]: Why did Gendou think Yui would be ok with him cheating?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Jun 15, 2014 11:59 pm I asked this a while ago but I figure I could have could have a new spin on the issue: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/8342/Why-was-Gendou-having-sex-with-the-Akagis/ I can understand that he was having sex with the Akagis because he wanted control over them, and having sex with them would help him with his plans. But did he think that when he would eventually see his wife again, she would understand or she would be ok with it? Just seems like defeating the whole purpose of trying to get back with Yui again.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Jun 16, 2014 12:12 am Sex is a biological thing, love isn't. I dunno, maybe he figured Yui would be beyond such petty things as jealousy once they both ascended to a higher plane of existence.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:02 am The affairs Gendo engaged in with both of the Akagi women might not have included any emotional component/investment on his part, but I'm sure Naoko and Ritsuko did not view their respective relationships with NERV's Commander in the same way. I think they not only allowed Gendo to completely manipulate them, but their shared obsession with him seemed to play a fairly large role toward ultimately corrupting both mother and daughter. Makes me wonder which behavior Yui would find more reprehensible; the actual adultery itself or the utter disregard displayed by her husband for the emotional scars he willingly inflicted (on multiple occasions toward multiple individuals) in the blind pursuit of accomplishing his own selfish goals. Then again, if you subscribe to the premise that she was in complete support of Gendo's cowardly plan to bail out of the Katsuragi Expedition the day before 2nd Impact, leaving everyone there to die without any attempt to either warn or save anyone, then it's probably a somewhat safe bet that her own personal moral compass hasn't been able to locate True North for quite a while. Might be a bit tough for her to cry foul with a straight face at this point in the game when she very well may have co-written Gendo's playbook.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:58 am BC Baron wrote: *too much text so snip*

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Jun 16, 2014 10:17 am Rosenakahara wrote: We are talking about the same woman who let Asuka die just to put Shinji (her own son) in a weakened emotional state so 3I could happen, she is already a terrible mother and person i do not think she would mind

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 16, 2014 10:28 am SEELE wrote: Interesting. Sadamoto did showed us that Shinji where able to call out for Unit01. I get your point but i said that to Bagheera recently. It is speculation with only a little amount of actual proof. Yeah the german dub translated "I can't. I can't pilot" and its more a resign than actual fear ... but still.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Jun 16, 2014 10:51 am Bagheera wrote: Sadamoto's story is a different continuity, so it doesn't mean anything. The proof is right there in the show. The timing of things, as well as the fact that Yui acted on her own, make the conclusion inescapable. It is very hard to square that interpretation with what she actually does in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 16, 2014 11:17 am SEELE wrote: About Sadamoto you are totally right. But he also interpret some things and it is valid to a certain point. I just rewatched the scene. The timing is right but this would doomed Re-Take and the hatredRetake!Asuka because this literally mean Asuka don't have common ground for accusation. But if i actually think about it - yeah i might conform to this. Yui-sama's masterplan (ahh, just f*k it). And the manga and Retake did also showed some perspective from Gendo. He is grieving.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 16, 2014 1:38 pm Frankly, it's not 'cheating' when she's dead, and Yui is the one who chose this outcome so she can't really blame the guy for finding release elsewhere. Considering she was willing to inflict this emotional trauma on her husband and son (and the implication that she knew damn well what would happen in 2I) I doubt very much she'd give a shit what happens to the Akagis. That's the downside of her whole 'anywhere can be a heaven' mantra... it means you can justify doing anything to other people because being happy with the situation is their concern, not yours.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 16, 2014 1:50 pm Related to the Yui tangent http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14738/Is-Yui-responsible-for-Asukas-death/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15498/http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15498/The-Sentience-of-the-Great-Yui-sama/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6262/Once-more-with-feeling-Yui-is-an-evil-bitch/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/703/Arch-Fiend-Yui-Inevitability-of-3I/

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 16, 2014 1:55 pm NemZ wrote: Frankly, it's not 'cheating' when she's dead, and Yui is the one who chose this outcome so she can't really blame the guy for finding release elsewhere. Considering she was willing to inflict this emotional trauma on her husband and son (and the implication that she knew damn well what would happen in 2I) I doubt very much she'd give a shit what happens to the Akagis. That's the downside of her whole 'anywhere can be a heaven' mantra... it means you can justify doing anything to other people because being happy with the situation is their concern, not yours.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 16, 2014 2:33 pm A First Wife and a series of concubines -- not an arrangement unknown to history. However dearly you might love your wife, if the physical side becomes impractical, there are still itches to be scratched. Applications for the post of Ritsuko can be made by PM using the button below.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Mon Jun 16, 2014 2:42 pm I've always maintained that Gendo was an extremely accomplished liar. Perhaps his greatest achievement with this particular skill might've been the self deception he performed throughout the series regarding his supposed feelings toward Yui and how he managed to convince himself that she somehow still harbored feelings toward him.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:07 pm Gendo and Yui are swingers.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Jun 16, 2014 3:10 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Gendo and Yui are swingers.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon Jun 16, 2014 4:29 pm Well she did bite him in half so maybe part of the reason she did it was because he cheated on her? I mean why not? He already done fucked up one thing, might as well have another reason for doing so.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Jun 16, 2014 5:28 pm SEELE wrote: Interesting. Sadamoto did showed us that Shinji where able to call out for Unit01. I get your point but i said that to Bagheera recently. It is speculation with only a little amount of actual proof. Yeah the german dub translated "I can't. I can't pilot" and its more a resign than actual fear ... but still.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 17, 2014 6:02 am Well, seeing as how Yui shows no evidence of being able to notice other human life-forms other than Shinji, I don’t have enough narrative data to assume that she willingly let Asuka die either. She only moves with no pilot in her twice in the whole series, and that’s not enough to conclude anything solidly. I mean, she doesn’t even move for Gendo either at any one point in the series (NGE, EoE, or NTE). Sure, he gets a rejection when he tries to stick her with “bondage gear” (the Dummy System) for a second time and force her to perform for him again, but even then all she might have recognized was a Dummy System, and not the man putting it in her. (It’s hard not to recognize something that’s being forced into your metaphorical womb.) So ultimately I can’t know enough to know whether or not she knew about anyone other than Shinji to begin with. There’s just not enough data. (Going even further on the whole womb concept, it could be that the only reason why she can recognize Shinji, and at times Rei, is because that is her own flesh and blood. It can be claimed, with little to no counterexamples, that Episode 13 proves this theory with the sync tests. Asuka is not physically related to her, so she may not even be able to perceive her to begin with. If Yui can’t perceive her to begin with, then she obviously can’t knowingly and willingly let her die.) This could also be why she never showed any grievance towards Gendo’s sexual activities while she was trapped in Unit 01. She simply didn’t know. Now why she seemed to not even be aware of it during Third Impact is kind of weird. I mean, all of the characters get to be in the head of all of the other characters, so it’s clear that she would have known by that point. Did she really think that little of Gendo, his sexual activities, or their relationship?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jun 18, 2014 6:54 am SEELE wrote: Interesting. Sadamoto did showed us that Shinji where able to call out for Unit01

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Wed Jun 18, 2014 7:20 am Kendrix wrote: But what applies in the manga needn't apply in the original.

Article: Why did Gendou think Yui would be ok with him cheating
 » Tue Jun 17, 2014 6:02 am Well, seeing as how Yui shows no evidence of being able to notice other human life-forms other than Shinji, I don’t have enough narrative data to assume that she willingly let Asuka die either. She only moves with no pilot in her twice in the whole series, and that’s not enough to conclude anything solidly. I mean, she doesn’t even move for Gendo either at any one point in the series (NGE, EoE, or NTE). Sure, he gets a rejection when he tries to stick her with “bondage gear” (the Dummy System) for a second time and force her to perform for him again, but even then all she might have recognized was a Dummy System, and not the man putting it in her. (It’s hard not to recognize something that’s being forced into your metaphorical womb.) So ultimately I can’t know enough to know whether or not she knew about anyone other than Shinji to begin with. There’s just not enough data. (Going even further on the whole womb concept, it could be that the only reason why she can recognize Shinji, and at times Rei, is because that is her own flesh and blood. It can be claimed, with little to no counterexamples, that Episode 13 proves this theory with the sync tests. Asuka is not physically related to her, so she may not even be able to perceive her to begin with. If Yui can’t perceive her to begin with, then she obviously can’t knowingly and willingly let her die.) This could also be why she never showed any grievance towards Gendo’s sexual activities while she was trapped in Unit 01. She simply didn’t know. Now why she seemed to not even be aware of it during Third Impact is kind of weird. I mean, all of the characters get to be in the head of all of the other characters, so it’s clear that she would have known by that point. Did she really think that little of Gendo, his sexual activities, or their relationship?
